# httptest

```go
import "net/http/httptest"
```

Package httptest provides utilities for HTTP testing.

## Constants

<a name="DefaultRemoteAddr"></a>DefaultRemoteAddr is the default remote address to return in RemoteAddr if an explicit DefaultRemoteAddr isn't set on [ResponseRecorder](<#ResponseRecorder>).

```go
const DefaultRemoteAddr = "1.2.3.4"
```

<a name="NewRequest"></a>

## func [NewRequest](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/httptest.go#L19>)

```go
func NewRequest(method, target string, body io.Reader) *http.Request
```

NewRequest wraps NewRequestWithContext using context.Background.

<a name="NewRequestWithContext"></a>

## func [NewRequestWithContext](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/httptest.go#L46>)

```go
func NewRequestWithContext(ctx context.Context, method, target string, body io.Reader) *http.Request
```

NewRequestWithContext returns a new incoming server Request, suitable for passing to an \[http.Handler\] for testing.

The target is the RFC 7230 "request\-target": it may be either a path or an absolute URL. If target is an absolute URL, the host name from the URL is used. Otherwise, "example.com" is used.

The TLS field is set to a non\-nil dummy value if target has scheme "https".

The Request.Proto is always HTTP/1.1.

An empty method means "GET".

The provided body may be nil. If the body is of type [bytes.Reader](<https://pkg.go.dev/bytes/#Reader>), [strings.Reader](<https://pkg.go.dev/strings/#Reader>), [bytes.Buffer](<https://pkg.go.dev/bytes/#Buffer>), or the value \[http.NoBody\], the Request.ContentLength is set.

NewRequest panics on error for ease of use in testing, where a panic is acceptable.

To generate a client HTTP request instead of a server request, see the NewRequest function in the net/http package.

<a name="ResponseRecorder"></a>

## type [ResponseRecorder](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/recorder.go#L21-L48>)

ResponseRecorder is an implementation of \[http.ResponseWriter\] that records its mutations for later inspection in tests.

```go
type ResponseRecorder struct {
    // Code is the HTTP response code set by WriteHeader.
    //
    // Note that if a Handler never calls WriteHeader or Write,
    // this might end up being 0, rather than the implicit
    // http.StatusOK. To get the implicit value, use the Result
    // method.
    Code int

    // HeaderMap contains the headers explicitly set by the Handler.
    // It is an internal detail.
    //
    // Deprecated: HeaderMap exists for historical compatibility
    // and should not be used. To access the headers returned by a handler,
    // use the Response.Header map as returned by the Result method.
    HeaderMap http.Header

    // Body is the buffer to which the Handler's Write calls are sent.
    // If nil, the Writes are silently discarded.
    Body *bytes.Buffer

    // Flushed is whether the Handler called Flush.
    Flushed bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
)

func main() {
	handler := func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		io.WriteString(w, "<html><body>Hello World!</body></html>")
	}

	req := httptest.NewRequest("GET", "http://example.com/foo", nil)
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	handler(w, req)

	resp := w.Result()
	body, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)

	fmt.Println(resp.StatusCode)
	fmt.Println(resp.Header.Get("Content-Type"))
	fmt.Println(string(body))

}
```

#### Output

```go
200
text/html; charset=utf-8
<html><body>Hello World!</body></html>
```

</p>
</details>

```go
func NewRecorder() *ResponseRecorder
```

NewRecorder returns an initialized [ResponseRecorder](<#ResponseRecorder>).

<a name="ResponseRecorder.Flush"></a>

### func \(\*ResponseRecorder\) [Flush](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/recorder.go#L181>)

```go
func (rw *ResponseRecorder) Flush()
```

<a name="ResponseRecorder.Header"></a>

### func \(\*ResponseRecorder\) [Header](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/recorder.go#L67>)

```go
func (rw *ResponseRecorder) Header() http.Header
```

Header implements \[http.ResponseWriter\]. It returns the response headers to mutate within a handler. To test the headers that were written after a handler completes, use the [ResponseRecorder.Result](<#ResponseRecorder.Result>) method and see the returned Response value's Header.

<a name="ResponseRecorder.Result"></a>

### func \(\*ResponseRecorder\) [Result](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/recorder.go#L203>)

```go
func (rw *ResponseRecorder) Result() *http.Response
```

Result returns the response generated by the handler.

The returned Response will have at least its StatusCode, Header, Body, and optionally Trailer populated. More fields may be populated in the future, so callers should not DeepEqual the result in tests.

The Response.Header is a snapshot of the headers at the time of the first write call, or at the time of this call, if the handler never did a write.

The Response.Body is guaranteed to be non\-nil and Body.Read call is guaranteed to not return any error other than [io.EOF](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#EOF>).

Result must only be called after the handler has finished running.

<a name="ResponseRecorder.Write"></a>

### func \(\*ResponseRecorder\) [Write](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/recorder.go#L107>)

```go
func (rw *ResponseRecorder) Write(buf []byte) (int, error)
```

Write implements http.ResponseWriter. The data in buf is written to rw.Body, if not nil.

<a name="ResponseRecorder.WriteString"></a>

### func \(\*ResponseRecorder\) [WriteString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/recorder.go#L121>)

```go
func (rw *ResponseRecorder) WriteString(str string) (int, error)
```

WriteString implements [io.StringWriter](<https://pkg.go.dev/io/#StringWriter>). The data in str is written to rw.Body, if not nil.

<a name="Server"></a>

## type [Server](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L27-L59>)

A Server is an HTTP server listening on a system\-chosen port on the local loopback interface, for use in end\-to\-end HTTP tests.

```go
type Server struct {
    URL      string // base URL of form http://ipaddr:port with no trailing slash
    Listener net.Listener

    // EnableHTTP2 controls whether HTTP/2 is enabled
    // on the server. It must be set between calling
    // NewUnstartedServer and calling Server.StartTLS.
    EnableHTTP2 bool

    // TLS is the optional TLS configuration, populated with a new config
    // after TLS is started. If set on an unstarted server before StartTLS
    // is called, existing fields are copied into the new config.
    TLS *tls.Config

    // Config may be changed after calling NewUnstartedServer and
    // before Start or StartTLS.
    Config *http.Server
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
)

func main() {
	ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Hello, client")
	}))
	defer ts.Close()

	res, err := http.Get(ts.URL)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	greeting, err := io.ReadAll(res.Body)
	res.Body.Close()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Printf("%s", greeting)
}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, client
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (HTT P2)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
)

func main() {
	ts := httptest.NewUnstartedServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		fmt.Fprintf(w, "Hello, %s", r.Proto)
	}))
	ts.EnableHTTP2 = true
	ts.StartTLS()
	defer ts.Close()

	res, err := ts.Client().Get(ts.URL)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	greeting, err := io.ReadAll(res.Body)
	res.Body.Close()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	fmt.Printf("%s", greeting)

}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, HTTP/2.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewServer"></a>

### func [NewServer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L106>)

```go
func NewServer(handler http.Handler) *Server
```

NewServer starts and returns a new [Server](<#Server>). The caller should call Close when finished, to shut it down.

<a name="NewTLSServer"></a>

### func [NewTLSServer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L218>)

```go
func NewTLSServer(handler http.Handler) *Server
```

NewTLSServer starts and returns a new [Server](<#Server>) using TLS. The caller should call Close when finished, to shut it down.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
)

func main() {
	ts := httptest.NewTLSServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		fmt.Fprintln(w, "Hello, client")
	}))
	defer ts.Close()

	client := ts.Client()
	res, err := client.Get(ts.URL)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	greeting, err := io.ReadAll(res.Body)
	res.Body.Close()
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Printf("%s", greeting)
}
```

#### Output

```go
Hello, client
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewUnstartedServer"></a>

### func [NewUnstartedServer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L118>)

```go
func NewUnstartedServer(handler http.Handler) *Server
```

NewUnstartedServer returns a new [Server](<#Server>) but doesn't start it.

After changing its configuration, the caller should call Start or StartTLS.

The caller should call Close when finished, to shut it down.

<a name="Server.Certificate"></a>

### func \(\*Server\) [Certificate](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L323>)

```go
func (s *Server) Certificate() *x509.Certificate
```

Certificate returns the certificate used by the server, or nil if the server doesn't use TLS.

<a name="Server.Client"></a>

### func \(\*Server\) [Client](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L333>)

```go
func (s *Server) Client() *http.Client
```

Client returns an HTTP client configured for making requests to the server. It is configured to trust the server's TLS test certificate and will close its idle connections on [Server.Close](<#Server.Close>). Use Server.URL as the base URL to send requests to the server. The returned client will also redirect any requests to "example.com" or its subdomains to the server.

<a name="Server.Close"></a>

### func \(\*Server\) [Close](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L230>)

```go
func (s *Server) Close()
```

Close shuts down the server and blocks until all outstanding requests on this server have completed.

<a name="Server.CloseClientConnections"></a>

### func \(\*Server\) [CloseClientConnections](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/net/http/httptest/server.go#L294>)

```go
func (s *Server) CloseClientConnections()
```

CloseClientConnections closes any open HTTP connections to the test Server.

<a name="Server.Start"></a>

```go
func (s *Server) Start()
```

Start starts a server from NewUnstartedServer.

<a name="Server.StartTLS"></a>

```go
func (s *Server) StartTLS()
```

StartTLS starts TLS on a server from NewUnstartedServer.
